_meta.title: RRF G-Code Dictionary
_meta.description: This file contains parsed documentation for functions. Do not remove this _meta block.

abs.title: Function
abs.description:
**Signature**:
`float->float`
`int->int`

Returns the absolute value of the operand

ceil.title: Function
ceil.description:
**Signature**:
`float->int`
`float->float`

Result is **int** if it fits in a 32-bit signed integer, else **float** (available in RRF 3.5.0 and later).

datetime.title: Function
datetime.description:
**Signature**:
`int->DateTime`
`string->DateTime`

Converts a number of seconds from the datum to a **DateTime**, or a string with format "yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss" to a **DateTime**. Available in RRF 3.4.0 and later.

drop.title: Function
drop.description:
**Signature**:
`(string, int)->string`
`(array, int)->array`

Returns all but the first N elements of the first argument, where N is the smaller of the second argument and the length of the first argument (available in RRF 3.6.0 and later).

exists.title: Function
exists.description:
**Signature**:
`name->bool`

Yields **true** if *name* is a valid variable or object model element name and is not null (available in RRF 3.3.0 and later). Especially useful for testing whether a particular parameter has been provided when a file macro was called.

exp.title: Function
exp.description:
**Signature**:
`float->float`

Returns *e* raised to the operand (supported in RRF 3.5.0 and later)

fileexists.title: Function
fileexists.description:
**Signature**:
`string->bool`

Yields **true** if the string parameter is the name of a file in the file system (available in RRF 3.5.0 and later).

fileread.title: Function
fileread.description:
**Signature**:
`(string, int, int, char)->array`

Returns an array of elements read from a single-line CSV or similar file (available in RRF 3.5.0 and later). The string parameter is the name of the file to read. The first integer parameter is the number of elements to skip; the second is the maximum number of elements to read; and the character is the field separator, typically ','. See note at the end of this table.

### Notes

Each element (including each skipped element) must be one of the following:

- a string in double quotes
- a number in any of the usual integer or floating point formats
- a character in single quotes
- empty (in which case a corresponding null element is included in the array).

Leading and trailing spaces and tabs around each element are ignored. If the file cannot be opened and read, or if any elements do not confirm to the above, then the command containing the fileread call will be aborted.

If the element is a quoted string, then characters within it that match the separator character are not treated as separators, and each consecutive pair of double-quote characters is replaced by a one double quote character.

The number of array elements returned will be one greater than the number of separator characters found; so an empty file will give rise to an array comprising a single null element.

find.title: Function
find.description:
**Signature**:
`(string, char)->int`
`(string, string)->int`

Returns the index of the first occurrence of the character in the string, or the index in the first string at which the first occurrence of the second string starts; or -1 if the second argument does not occur in the first argument (available in RRF 3.6.0 and later).

floor.title: Function
floor.description:
**Signature**:
`float->int`
`float->float`

Result is **int** if it fits in a 32-bit signed integer, else **float**

isnan.title: Function
isnan.description:
**Signature**:
`float->bool`

Returns **true** if the operand is a NaN (Not-a-Number) e.g. *sqrt(-1)*

log.title: Function
log.description:
**Signature**:
`float->float`

Returns the natural logarithm of the operand (supported in RRF 3.5.0 and later)

max.title: Function
max.description:
**Signature**:
`(float, ...)->float`
`(int, ...)->int`

Accepts 1 or more arguments. If any argument is NaN then the result is NaN.

min.title: Function
min.description:
**Signature**:
`(float, ...)->float`
`(int, ...)->int`

Accepts 1 or more arguments. If any argument is NaN then the result is NaN.

mod.title: Function
mod.description:
**Signature**:
`(int, int)->int`
`(float, float)->float`

Returns the remainder from dividing the first operand by the second operand

pow.title: Function
pow.description:
**Signature**:
`(float, float)->float`
`(int, int)->int`

Returns the first operand to the power of the second operand  (supported in RRF 3.5.0 and later). The result type is **int** if the operands are int, the second operand is non-negative, and the result fits in an **int**.

random.title: Function
random.description:
**Signature**:
`int->int`

Operand must >= 1. Returns a pseudo-random integer in the range 0 to one less than the operand.

### Notes

The **random** function uses the hardware true random number generator if the microcontroller provides one. If the microcontroller doesn't provide one then an algorithmic pseudo random number generator is seeded by the system tick counter the first time it is called. The Duet 3 6HC, 6XD and Mini 5+ boards all use microcontrollers that provide a true random number generator.

round.title: Function
round.description:
**Signature**:
`float->int`
`float->float`

Rounds to nearest integer, or to nearest even integer if the fractional part of the operand is exactly one half. Result is **int** if it fits in a 32-bit signed integer, else **float** (available in RRF 3.6.0 and later).

sqrt.title: Function
sqrt.description:
**Signature**:
`float->float`

Returns the square root of the operand

square.title: Function
square.description:
**Signature**:
`float->float`

Returns the square of the operand (available in RRF 3.6.0 and later)

take.title: Function
take.description:
**Signature**:
`(string, int)->string`
`(array, int)->array`

Returns the first N elements of the first argument, where N is the smaller of the second argument and the length of the first argument (available in RRF 3.6.0 and later)

vector.title: Function
vector.description:
**Signature**:
`(int, X)->array`

(RRF 3.5.0 and later) Returns an array with the number of elements equal to the first operand and each element a copy of the second operand
